publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday December 2, 2025 at 22:00

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: Gary C. Burget, M.D. (1941-2017) was a plastic surgeon specializing in facial and nasal reconstruction. Dr. Burget attended Yale University as an undergraduate and went on to graduate from Yale School of Medicine in 1967 before entering private practice first in Miami and then in Chicago. The collection mainly consists of patient records, which include patient charts, 35mm slides, photographs, x-rays, photographic negatives, illustrations, and consultation records. Also included in the collection are personal and professional correspondence, photographs, illustrations, notes, and 35mm slides from his publications, lectures, and conferences, as well as his medical volunteer trips to Southeast Asia and South America.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Materials that do not contain patients' medical information are open for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient records and materials containing patient medical information are restricted until 2067.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in five series: Series I. Teaching and conferences, 1972-2009. Series II. Writing and publishing, 1962-2012. Series III. International volunteer work, 1980-1989. Series IV. Personal papers, 1964-2015. Series V. Patient records, 1963-2017.
Series I. is further arranged in three subseries: Correspondence, 1995 – 2006; Educational material, 1972 – 2009; and Teaching ephemera, 1991-2002.
Series III. is further arranged in two subseries: Patient files, 1980 1989, and Travel, circa 1980-1989.
Series IV. is further arranged in five subseries: Correspondence, circa 1982 – 2015; Education, 1968 – 1974; Genealogy, circa 1970-1979; Societies, 1982 – 2004; and Travel, 1964 – 1983.

Series V. is further arranged in seven subseries: Patient files, 1963 – 2017; Patient negatives, 1979 – 2012; Reconstructive cases, 1976 – 2000; Ear reconstruction, 1978 – 1990; Facial reconstruction, 1974 – 1997; Patient consults, 1987 – 2017; and Audiovisual materials, 1963 - 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials received from Burget without apparent order have been arranged by alphabetic order, date, and/or subject.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Gary C. Burget, M.D. (1941-2017) was a plastic surgeon specializing in facial and nasal reconstruction. Born in Toledo, Ohio in 1941, Dr. Burget attended Yale University earning his undergraduate degree in English and went on to attend Yale School of Medicine, graduating in 1967. He obtained his medical license in 1968 and continued his education with an internship at Columbia University College of Physicians and Surgeons (1969), a residency at Jackson Memorial Hospital in Miami (1972), and a plastic surgery fellowship with Dr. Ralph Millard in Miami (1974). He entered private practice first in Miami, Florida and then in Chicago, Illinois.

In the 1980s Burget spent time traveling and volunteering at hospitals in Southeast Asia, Korea, and South America consulting on plastic surgery cases, most frequently in Thailand and in South Korea. As a pioneer in the forehead flap surgical technique for nasal reconstruction, Dr. Burget spoke at surgical teaching conferences and was a guest lecturer at universities and hospitals in the United States and abroad throughout his career.

In addition to his medical practice and teaching, Burget wrote about pioneering techniques within the field of plastic surgery. Burget's publications include: Aesthetic Reconstruction of the Nose published in the 1994, co-authored with Dr. Fredrick Menick, and Aesthetic Reconstruction of the Child's Nose , published in 2005. He published many other articles regarding new techniques in the surgical world, with specific focus on facial surgery.

Dr. Burget engaged in the community of medicine, maintaining membership in many professional organizations including, but not limited to, The American Society for Aesthetic Plastic Surgery, Chicago Society of Plastic Surgery, and The Rhinoplasty Society. In 1990 and 2008 he won the James Barrett-Brown Award for the best plastic surgery paper published during the previous calendar year. In 2006, Dr. Burget was named by the American Association of Plastic Surgeons as its "Clinician of the Year."
After practicing in Chicago for more than 30 years, he retired from practice in October 2016. Gary C. Burget died on May 31, 2017, in Chicago, Illinois.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gary C. Burget papers. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing/John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The papers contain records that document Dr. Gary C. Burget's (1941-2017) plastic surgery education, plastic surgery career and medical practice, teaching, conference activity, as well as a small part of his personal correspondence and travels from 1962 - 2017. The collection includes correspondence, manuscripts, publications, patient records, photographs, slides, and video tapes. Correspondence includes letters from Dr. Robert W. Berliner (1915-2002) and Dr. Frederick J. Menick regarding Burget's teaching, conference activities, and publications. Certificates and awards won throughout his career are included in the collection and include society awards, such as the Fellow award from the American College of Surgeons, and various other medical and personal societies to which he belonged. Records relating to his surgical education include certificates and diplomas from his various residencies including the Harlem Hospital Center in New York City, New York and the Jackson Memorial Hospital in Miami, Florida. Records relating to his surgical career are the majority of the collection and consist of surgical patient records: patient charts, 35mm slides, photographs, x-rays, negatives, and consults created in Miami and Chicago. 35mm slides and photographs are present throughout the collection, documenting patients' pre-, intra-, and post- operative conditions and surgical procedures. Photographs, slides, x-rays, and negatives within patient charts were kept in context and 35mm slides, photographs, and negatives maintained in separate files by Burget may be found in multiple sub-series. Dr. Burget specialized in facial surgery and nasal reconstructive surgery, but patient records may include treatment, surgeries, and consultations about a range of plastic surgery areas. The illustrations and artwork created in the scope of his practice, teaching, and in publishing his books include works by Todd Buck and other artists. The illustrations and artwork include oversized material, illustrations, pencil on paper, paint on paper, ink on paper, negatives, and hand sketches. Materials documenting Burget's teaching and conference activity throughout the United States and around the world as well as his international volunteerism work in Southeast Asia, Korea, and South America include manuscripts, personal and professional correspondence, illustrations, photographs, notes, and slides.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Gary C. Burget. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

corpname.part: Columbia University. College of Physicians and Surgeons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides, illustrations, correspondence, and ephemera concerning conferences, lectures, workshops, and teaching engagements attended throughout his medical career.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into two subseries.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This series contains material that has patient health information. Patient records and materials containing patient medical information are restricted. Contact the Medical Historical Library to request access.
Series I: Box 5 has restricted material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes emails, letters, photographs, invitations, travel documents, and ephemera between Burget, his office, and those he was in communication with regarding his lectures and teaching engagements at universities, conferences, and medical associations around the world.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arrangement is chronological by year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides, illustrations, negatives, drawings, internegatives, and transparencies. Format of illustrations and drawings are: pencil on paper, ink on paper, pencil on tissue, paint on paper, pencil/ink/paint on paper, ink on transparencies, and internegatives. While some of the work is identified by the artist, many of the illustrations and drawings are unattributed and undated.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Slide material is organized by lecture subject. Slides containing patient medical information are restricted. Art and illustrations are in alphabetical order by content depicted. There are oversized materials in this subseries.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient files and materials containing patient medical information are restricted. Contact the Medical Historical Library to request access.
Series I: Box 5 has restricted material.

did.unittitle: Cheek flap on male face printed drawing
Michael Carroll illustrations face anatomy on female
Michael Carroll illustrations flap and ABBE
Michael Carroll illustrations nasal incision and fibro-fatty tissue removed
Michael Carroll illustrations nose flap series

Todd Buck illustrations figure 1A through figure 7

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient records and materials containing patient medical information are restricted. Contact the Medical Historical Library to request access.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Material in Box 5 are restricted due to patient health information.

did.unittitle: Noli Me Tangere technique "Exposure Free Technique"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes awards and certificates given for Burget's guest lectures and teaching appearances.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material was arranged in alphabetical order.

did.unittitle: Baylor College of Medicine visiting professor certificate
Visiting Professor advertisement for Bogota, Columbia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series includes notes, drafts, illustrations, published copies of articles and books, and correspondence concerning medical procedures, techniques, and expertise throughout Burget's medical career. The series begins during his time as an undergraduate at Yale University in the 1960s and ends with his last major published work in 2012. Also included are two publications by his brother, Dean E. Burget, from the time when they were both at Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged by publication title and year.

title.part: Identification of the recurrent laryngeal nerve
Eli Ives of New Haven and Yale
Nasal shadows and nasal scars deceiving the eye in reconstruction of the nose

did.unittitle: Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter one (Old Manuscript)
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter one With figures
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter two (early manuscript)
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter one and two
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter three
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter three original drawings
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter four
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter five
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter nine (earlier manuscript)
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter ten draft
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter ten original drawings
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter eleven figures
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter eleven and old manuscripts
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter twelve and thirteen
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter fourteen
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose chapter fourteen figures
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose loose illustrations
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose illustrations
Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose slides

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient records and materials containing patient medical information are restricted. Contact the Medical Historical Library to request access.

did.unittitle: Aesthetic reconstruction of the nose correspondence
Aesthetic and functional nasal reconstruction illustration list

title.part: Optimal use of microvascular free flaps, cartilage grafts, and a paramedian forehead flap for aesthetic reconstruction of the nose and adjacent facial units
Preliminary review of pediatric nasal reconstruction with detailed report of one case

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs, slides, correspondence, travel ephemera, and patient files concerning his time spent traveling to other countries to perform surgical procedures at hospitals as a visiting expert and lend his expertise and knowledge during the 1980s. Includes material from Korea, the Philippines, Thailand, and Belize. It is unclear if he was compensated for this work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Include slides, photographs, and notes from the patients seen during his missionary work, many of whom were children. The majority of the photographic slides are from his time in Korea and the majority of the photographs are from his time in Southeast Asia. Names and procedures are included on many of the slides and photographs, many of the photographs have labels with the information as well.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged by geographical location and year.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient records and materials containing patient medical information are restricted.
Series III: Box 10 and Box 11 are restricted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters, photographs, slides, and ephemera from the 1980s when he traveled for surgical missionary work and includes letters and photographs from those he met and worked with while on his trips, unrestricted slides from the hospitals he worked at in Korea, and travel ephemera from Thailand.

did.unittitle: The Balikbayan Medical Mission to the southern Philippines essay
Observational slides of hospital and travel around Korea
Philippines hospital staff with Gary Burget

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes research, slides, photographs, ephemera, correspondence, diplomas, and awards relating to Burget's personal travels, family history, undergraduate degree and medical school achievements, relations with family members and travel companions, and various groups and societies, both international and local from 1964 to 2015.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged into five subseries based on subject matter. When possible, original order was adhered to.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters, cards, photographs, and ephemera from family members, colleagues, and travel companions from 1982 – 2001. Burget family photographs are included.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Subseries is arranged in chronological order.

did.unittitle: Portrait and negative of unidentified friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes educational and vocational certificates related to his medical education in becoming a plastic surgeon as well as his certificate as a visiting professor in Texas. The material also includes his medical licenses for New York State, California, and Florida as well as his certificates for residency in general surgery and plastic surgery in Harlem, New York City and Miami, Florida.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The subseries is arranged chronologically by year.

did.unittitle: Jackson Memorial Hospital resident in plastic surgery certificate
Jackson Memorial Hospital resident in general surgery certificate
License to practice medicine and surgery in the state of Florida
Physician's and surgeon's certificate from the state of California
Resident surgery at Harlem Hospital Center
License to practice medicine and surgery in the state of New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes research for Burget and Allwine family history including family trees with names and dates going back to 1649, ancestors in the Blue Book of Philadelphia Furniture , marriage certificates, and explanations of relationships between family members with notable names including Blackburn, Armstrong, Allwine, and Burget.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This subseries is arranged in alphabetical order by subject.

did.unittitle: Burget and Allwine family from 1711 to 1963
The Blue Book of Philadelphia Furniture 1682-1807 , page 300-321

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes pamphlets, certificates, and photographs regarding his personal interests and professional work. Includes material from the Genealogical Society of the Huguenots, international and local surgical societies, certificates from foreign hospitals and medical associations, speaking certificates, and medical associations. Included is a copy of Burget's birth certificate.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The subseries is arranged in alphabetical order.

did.unittitle: The American Society for Aesthetic Plastic Surgery, Inc.
Israel Society of Plastic & Aesthetic Surgery
New England Society of Plastic & Reconstructive Surgeons, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes slides and photographs from Burget's personal travels in Asia, Europe, and North America from 1964 – 1983. Notable places include London, Paris, Edinburgh, and Asia.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The subseries is arranged in chronological order.

did.unittitle: Sailing and fishing in North America

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series includes paper charts, photographs, 35mm slides, negatives, x-rays, digital objects, and some audiovisual material concerning Burget's medical practice from 1963 to 2017. The majority of the series consists of patient charts. There are two types of charts; older charts which consist of a folder with all information in date order and newer style charts, which separate notes, insurance information, and test results into different sections of the file folder. Most charts include photographs and slides and a small number contain x-rays. Consultation records range from one page of inquiries to charts containing patient information, email correspondence, and photographs. There are also slides, loose photographs, and negatives within the series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The arrangement of the series reflects Burget's filing order. Patient charts, photographs, and negatives are arranged in alphabetic order with subseries for reconstructive cases, facial reconstructive cases, ear reconstructive cases, patient consults, and negatives. Audiovisual materials have been arranged in a separate subseries. Records for a single patient may exist in multiple subseries.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Patient names in this finding aid have been replaced with alphanumeric codes to protect privacy. The code for each subseries begins with a letter indicating first letter of last name, followed by two letters indicating format, then a four digit number starting with 0001.

[Alphabetical Letter]ch# - Charts [Alphabetical Letter]sl# - Slides [Alphabetical Letter]ph# - Photographs [Alphabetical Letter]co# - Consults [Alphabetical Letter]rc# - Reconstructive Cases [Alphabetical Letter]fr# - Facial Reconstruction [Alphabetical Letter]er# - Ear Reconstruction

Similar numbers in different subseries do not represent the same patient names. For example: Patient charts Ach0001 and Patient slides Asl0001 are not necessarily the same patient. A spreadsheet linking the alphanumeric codes to patient names is held by the repository.

accessrestrict.head: Restricted
accessrestrict.p: Patient Records and materials containing patient medical information are restricted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of records of Burget's patients throughout his career including patient charts, photographic prints, photographic slides, negatives, and x-rays. Patient photographs include loose photographs originally in white paper envelopes stored separately from the patient charts. Some photographs were accompanied by handwritten notes and personal photographs of the patients. Some photographs correlate to patient charts but many do not.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The patient files are arranged alphabetically by last name, first name across every physical type of material.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Patient's names have been replaced with alphanumeric codes to protect patient health information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient files and materials containing patient medical information are restricted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes negative strips and original envelopes from 1979 to 2012.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The negatives are arranged chronologically with multiple patients included in one enclosure as received from Burget.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient Records and materials containing patient medical information are restricted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 35 mm photographic slides of pre-, inter-, and post-operative images of reconstructive surgery cases. Burget stored these slides separately from other patient records.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by patient's last name, first name.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Patient names have been replaced with a three letter code and a number to protect patient health information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient files and materials containing patient medical information are restricted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 35mm photographic slides of pre-, inter-, and post-operative ear reconstruction that Burget kept separated from other patient records.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by patient last name, first name.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Patient's names have been replaced with a three letter code and a number to protect patient health information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient Records and materials containing patient medical information are restricted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 35mm photographic slides of pre-, inter-, and post-operative of facial reconstruction surgeries Burget kept separated from other patient records.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by last name, first name.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Patient's names have been replaced with a three letter code and a number to protect patient health information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient Records and materials containing patient medical information are restricted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of consult records for patients Burget may or may not have ended up treating. Consults include children and adults from the United States and other countries and may include slides, radiology reports, insurance forms, ingest paperwork, and correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged in alphabetical order by patient name or name of consulting physician with multiple records in each folder.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Patient's names have been replaced with a three letter code and a number to protect patient health information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient Records and materials containing patient medical information are restricted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes microform duplicate patient records, CD-ROMs containing patient health information; and VHS tapes.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patient Records and materials containing patient medical information are restricted.
